  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Drywall joints are not staggered between adjacent boards, creating continuous vertical or horizontal seams."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Remove non-compliant drywall sheets and reinstall them ensuring joints are staggered by at least one stud spacing (typically 16 or 24 inches) per GA-216."
  ],
  "rationale": "Both GA-216 (Specifications for the Application and Finishing of Gypsum Panel Products) and the IRC require that joints of gypsum board be staggered on opposite sides of a partition, and that adjacent board joints on the same face be staggered to prevent cracking and maintain structural/fire-resistance integrity.",
  "references": [
    "GA-216 Section 4.7",
    "IRC Section R702.3.5"
  ]
}
